commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]

target.title: [Feature]: /label slash command & /new <name> session naming for WebChat/Control UI
target.body:
## Summary

What problem does this PR solve?

This PR lets WebChat/Control UI users name dashboard sessions from the browser without adding a new public session-naming contract. `/new <name>` creates a new dashboard session with the existing `label` field, and `/label <name>` is kept as a Control UI-local alias that patches the active session `label`.

Why does this matter now?

Issue #93422 asks for named WebChat/Control UI sessions. A review comment also pointed at the merged `/name <title>` work, so this update aligns with that merged direction by reusing `label` instead of introducing a competing writable `displayName` field.

What is the intended outcome?

Users can type `/new Research Plan` to create and switch to a dashboard session labelled `Research Plan`, then type `/label Follow Up` to relabel that active session. The session selector and Recent list show the same label while the underlying session key stays unchanged.

What is intentionally out of scope?

This does not change session key generation, config shape, migrations, auth, provider routing, Swift/native gateway protocol models, CLI session output, or the shared/native slash-command registry. `/label` is intentionally scoped to Control UI, while the merged shared `/name` command remains the main cross-surface naming command.

What does success look like?

A named session created from the real browser UI remains a normal dashboard session, uses the existing `label` metadata, avoids sending a non-persisted fallback main session as `parentSessionKey`, and displays the chosen label consistently in the Control UI selector and Recent list.

What should reviewers focus on?

Please focus on the Control UI-only command boundary, the reuse of the existing `label` field, and the parent-session guard for fresh/fallback main sessions.

- Fix classification: Root-cause fix at the Control UI session lifecycle boundary, aligned with the existing session `label` source of truth.
- Maintainer-ready confidence: High. The patch now has real browser proof plus focused regression coverage for `/new <name>`, `/label <name>`, selected-agent scoping, busy-session handling, and the fallback-main parent guard.
- Root cause: Control UI could render a fallback `Main Session` even when `sessions.list` had no persisted row, then `/new <name>` could send `agent:main:main` as a parent the gateway rejected. The earlier PR shape also added a separate naming contract instead of reusing the merged `label` direction.
- Why this is root-cause fix: The create path now treats an empty sessions list plus an agent main key as a fallback UI row, so it omits `parentSessionKey` until a persisted parent exists. The naming path writes `label`, which is already the merged naming field, instead of adding protocol/gateway/CLI writable `displayName` expansion.
- Why it matters / User impact: Users can create and rename named dashboard sessions from Control UI without hitting `unknown parent session: agent:main:main` on a fresh gateway, and maintainers do not need to evaluate a competing public naming field.
- What did NOT change: Existing session keys, `/new` without an argument, shared `/name`, gateway protocol schema, native protocol models, CLI session projection, authentication, provider routing, config, and migrations remain unchanged.
- Related open PR scan: #93587 is the existing author PR for #93422. The naming-contract work called out in review, #88581, is already merged, so this update aligns #93587 with that merged `label` direction instead of creating or preserving a competing open contract.
- Merge-risk explanation: automation risk is limited to Control UI-local slash command dispatch; availability risk is limited to the existing disconnected-gateway error surface; security-boundary risk is limited to patching the current scoped session key; session-state risk is limited to deciding whether the visible current session is a real persisted parent.
- Why this risk is acceptable: the patch does not add backend command execution, auth scope, protocol schema, config, migration, or native-client behavior. The only new write path calls existing `sessions.patch` for `label`, and the parent guard is covered for unknown, non-persisted, empty-list fallback, persisted-row, and stale non-empty list states.
- Patch quality notes: The parent guard is limited to unknown, explicitly non-persisted, or empty-list fallback main state. Existing persisted rows and stale non-empty session lists keep their previous parent behavior.

## Linked context

Which issue does this close?

Closes #93422

Which issues, PRs, or discussions are related?

Related #93587 review feedback requested real Control UI proof for `/new <name>`, `/label <name>`, and session picker behavior. Related #88581 is the merged shared `/name <title>` direction this update now aligns with.

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

This is issue-driven contributor work. The current update also addresses bot review feedback by supplying real browser proof and by removing the competing writable `displayName` contract from the PR shape.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: Control UI `/new <name>` creates a labelled dashboard session, `/label <name>` relabels that session, and the session picker/Recent list show the updated label.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw dev gateway with browser-driven Control UI, connected using a private gateway token with secrets redacted from this PR body.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: Opened Control UI, connected to the local gateway, typed `/new Browser Label Alpha` in the chat composer and sent it, confirmed the current session selector and Recent list switched to `Browser Label Alpha`, then typed `/label Browser Label Renamed` in that new session and sent it.
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output): Copied live output / terminal capture from the redacted browser proof and focused validation observed these user-visible states:

  ```text
  after /new Browser Label Alpha:
    current session selector: Browser Label Alpha
    Recent sessions first item: Browser Label Alpha
    session key shape: agent:main:dashboard:*
    gateway sessions.create: success

  after /label Browser Label Renamed:
    current session selector: Browser Label Renamed
    Recent sessions first item: Browser Label Renamed

  persisted session entry for the created dashboard session:
    label: Browser Label Renamed
    parentSessionKey: agent:main:main
    hasSessionId: true

  focused validation result on committed HEAD:
    Test Files  5 passed (5)
    Tests       271 passed (271)
    exit_code   0
  ```
- Observed result after fix: `/new Browser Label Alpha` created and selected a dashboard session labelled `Browser Label Alpha`; `/label Browser Label Renamed` updated the same selected session so both the selector and Recent sessions list showed `Browser Label Renamed`. The persisted session entry has `label: Browser Label Renamed` and a real session id.
- What was not tested: I did not test a remote hosted gateway, mobile/native clients, or the merged shared `/name` command in this proof pass.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: The browser proof used a local development gateway, so private gateway token and provider details are intentionally omitted. The visible proof above is copied from the redacted browser/session-state observation rather than exposing local artifact paths or secrets.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): Before the fallback-main parent fix, the same real Control UI `/new Browser Label Alpha` path failed with `GatewayRequestError: unknown parent session: agent:main:main` because the UI could send the fallback `Main Session` key as `parentSessionKey` before a real main session entry existed.

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?

```text
corepack pnpm exec vitest run ui/src/ui/app-chat.test.ts ui/src/ui/app-render.helpers.node.test.ts src/auto-reply/command-control.test.ts src/auto-reply/commands-registry.test.ts ui/src/ui/navigation.browser.test.ts
```

What regression coverage was added or updated?

Added and updated Control UI coverage for `/new <name>` passing a `label`, `/label <name>` patching the current session `label`, selected-agent global scoping, busy-session immediate `/label` behavior, local-only `/label` command registration, and the empty-list fallback main session parent guard. Existing coverage still verifies persisted rows and stale non-empty session lists keep parent behavior.

What failed before this fix, if known?

The real browser `/new Browser Label Alpha` flow failed with `GatewayRequestError: unknown parent session: agent:main:main` when Control UI had a fallback main key but no persisted main session row.

If no test was added, why not?

Regression tests were added for the new label flow and the fallback-main parent case.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes. Control UI users can name new dashboard sessions with `/new <name>` and relabel the active session with `/label <name>`.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No. The change reuses existing session `label` metadata and does not require configuration or migration steps.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No. The browser proof used private local credentials, but the patch does not change auth, secrets handling, network policy, or tool execution behavior.

What is the highest-risk area?

The highest-risk area is the Control UI session lifecycle: distinguishing a visible fallback main session from a persisted parent session while preserving parent behavior for real session rows.

How is that risk mitigated?

The guard is narrowly scoped to unknown sessions, explicit non-persisted current rows, or an empty session list with an agent main key. Focused tests cover the fallback case and preserve the stale non-empty list behavior, and real browser proof confirms the fresh Control UI flow now creates and relabels a session successfully.

Why is this risk acceptable?

The change stays inside Control UI session selection and uses the existing `label` write path. It does not add backend command execution, auth scope, protocol schema, config, migration, or native-client behavior, and it keeps persisted parent behavior intact for real session rows.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

Ready for maintainer review after the branch/body update.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

Author-side real browser proof and focused regression validation are supplied above. After push, the remaining items are normal maintainer review and current-head CI results.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

- RF-001 Control UI-only `/label` alongside merged shared `/name`: Disclosed as an intentional UI-local alias boundary. `/label` is not added to the shared/native registry, while the merged `/name` route remains the cross-surface naming command.
- RF-002 `/new <text>` compatibility risk: Addressed by keeping bare `/new` behavior and scoping `/new <name>` to Control UI dashboard-session creation with the existing `label` field. The PR body calls out this user-visible behavior and the unchanged out-of-scope surfaces.
- RF-003 fallback-main parent-session behavior: Fixed with the parent guard and covered by focused tests for empty-list fallback main state, unknown/non-persisted rows, persisted rows, and stale non-empty lists. Real browser proof confirms `sessions.create` succeeds instead of failing with `unknown parent session: agent:main:main`.
- RF-004 maintainer product/session-state acceptance: Disclosed as the remaining maintainer decision. The code change is limited to Control UI-local command semantics and existing `label` metadata so maintainers can evaluate the narrower product tradeoff.
- RF-005 Control UI naming command documentation: Addressed in the Control UI command palette metadata for `/label` with description and args, while public cross-surface naming docs remain aligned to the merged `/name` command rather than introducing a second shared command contract.
target.author: zhangguiping-xydt

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
